title: Only open save output file with WRONLY
body:
The previous code fails on a MAC when opening /dev/stdout

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12402

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] No easy way to test this.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Yes, this is what I think is going wrong and how to fix it, but please wait for a confirmation from someone who has Podman remote actually set up.
I think this is only podman on a MAC too, correct.  I could not get this to fail on Linux.

It might be possible to reproduce on Linux using something like
```sh
touch output;
chmod 0200 output # u=w,u-r
podman save $other-args -o output # Must be podman-remote!
```
where the `-o output` is new, to prevent triggering the (Linux-only) https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7324d94648a9987b0de2dc95cf1b6fbc20592532/cmd/podman/images/save.go#L115 code. But that’s just reading the code, I could easily be missing something.

Works for me. I checked out this branch, built it, and ran it on my Mac, comparing the behavior to the Homebrew-installed version:

```
❯ ./bin/darwin/podman save centos:7 | sha256sum
08129aca6f66e3aa07368ffb8576e6612b67bd1ca624a7fd155d03aa9608ee8a  -
❯ podman save centos:7 | sha256sum
Error: open /dev/stdout: permission denied
```
